skill.name: gws-sheets-read
skill.description: Read cell values from Google Sheets using the gws command line tool.
skill.summary: Read a selected range from a Google Sheets spreadsheet.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, path traversal, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown command examples and sibling documentation links rather than executable code or cryptographic logic. The skill is read-only and does not include scripts, network code, or prompt injection text. Low risk remains because it instructs users to run the gws CLI against Google Sheets data and references files outside the skill directory.

low_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Required
low_findings.description: The skill documents gws command usage for reading spreadsheet values. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but users should understand that it depends on an installed CLI and Google Workspace authorization.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct evidence that the skill expects external gws CLI execution. The examples are static documentation and do not show shell metacharacters, dynamic command construction, or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Sibling Documentation References
low_findings.description: The skill references sibling markdown files with relative parent paths. These are documentation links, not runtime file reads, but they should be packaged or reviewed with the related shared instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences are visible in markdown references and a prerequisite sentence. They are not used in executable code, but they do point outside the skill directory.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are normal description and table text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain the skill description and a markdown table header. There is no cryptographic operation or algorithm selection in the skill content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples for the gws CLI. This is expected for a command helper skill and does not indicate command injection by itself.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are clearly fenced shell snippets with placeholder arguments. No untrusted interpolation or automatic execution is present in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Parent Path References
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill links to sibling skill documentation using parent directory paths. This is a packaging and review concern, not direct path traversal exploitation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The parent path markers are present, but only inside markdown documentation links. There is no code that reads arbitrary user-controlled paths.

content.value_statement: Teams often need quick access to spreadsheet values without opening the browser. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to read selected Google Sheets ranges through the gws CLI.
actual_capabilities: Shows the required gws command for reading spreadsheet values.
Identifies the required spreadsheet ID flag.
Identifies the required range flag for A1 notation or sheet names.
Provides examples for reading a cell range or a full sheet.
States that the command is read-only and does not modify the spreadsheet.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and configured.
Requires valid Google Workspace authentication and spreadsheet access.
Does not document formatting, formulas, or write operations.
Advanced options require using the raw values.get API.

use_cases.title: Pull Weekly Metrics
use_cases.description: Read a fixed reporting range from a shared spreadsheet for summaries or checks.

use_cases.title: Inspect Shared Data
use_cases.description: Retrieve selected rows or columns before preparing a response or document.

use_cases.title: Verify Spreadsheet Inputs
use_cases.description: Read source sheet values before running a downstream workflow or analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Read One Range
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a known spreadsheet and range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gws-sheets-read to read spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID from range Sheet1!A1:D10.

prompt_templates.title: Read A Full Sheet
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic request when the user wants all visible data from one sheet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the Google Sheets spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID using range Sheet1, then summarize the returned values.

prompt_templates.title: Check Data Before Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that reads values before deciding next steps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read range Data!A1:H50 from spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID. Identify missing values and columns that need cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Reporting Sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using multiple reads in a review workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read ranges January!A1:F30 and February!A1:F30 from spreadsheet SPREADSHEET_ID. Compare totals, missing rows, and changed labels.

output_examples.output: The assistant runs the read command, then reports the rows returned and any empty cells in the requested range.

output_examples.input: Read the full Budget sheet and summarize it.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves values from the named sheet and provides a concise summary of categories, totals, and notable gaps.

output_examples.input: Compare two monthly ranges in the same spreadsheet.
output: It compares matching labels and totals.
It highlights differences that need review.

best_practices: Confirm the spreadsheet ID and range before running the command.
Use the smallest range that contains the needed data.
Keep spreadsheet access limited to users and service accounts that need it.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for write, update, or delete operations.
Do not request broad sheet ranges when a smaller range is enough.
Do not expose spreadsheet IDs or returned values in public logs.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Google Sheets?
faq.answer: No. The documented command reads values only and does not change the spreadsheet.

faq.question: What inputs are required?
faq.answer: You need a spreadsheet ID and a range, such as Sheet1!A1:B2 or Sheet1.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The gws CLI must be installed and authenticated with access to the spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can it read an entire sheet?
faq.answer: Yes. The example shows using a sheet name as the range.

faq.question: Can it read formulas or formatting?
faq.answer: This skill only documents value reads. Advanced behavior requires the raw values.get API.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
